quality.rationale: 球对称流体坍缩模型结合爱因斯坦场方程多步微积分推导

origin.question:
Problem 16.26. If $\mathbf{R}$ is a comoving coordinate, the metric for a spherically symmetric collapsing star (see Problem 16.25) can be written as
$$
\mathrm {d s}^{2} = - \mathrm {e}^{2 \Phi} \mathrm {d t}^{2} + \mathrm {e}^{2 \Lambda} \mathrm {d R}^{2} + \mathrm {r}^{2} (\mathrm {t}, \mathrm {R}) \mathrm {d}\Omega^{2}
$$
where $\Phi$ and $\Lambda$ are functions of $\mathbb{R}$ , t. If the star is made of a perfect fluid it is often useful to define the following functions:
$$
\begin{array}{l}
 m \equiv \int_{0}^{R} 4 \pi r^{2} \rho r^{\prime} d R \\
 \mathrm {U} \equiv \mathrm {e}^{- \Phi} \dot {r} \\
 \Gamma^{2} \equiv e^{- 2 \Lambda} (r^{\prime})^{2}.
 \end{array}
$$
Here primes denote partial differentiation with respect to $\mathbf{R}$ , and dots with respect to $t$ . The function $m$ is interpreted as the mass interior to the shell at $\mathbf{R}$ , and $U$ is the rate at which a shell is moving with respect to the proper time of a comoving observer.
Prove the following relations:
$$
\Gamma^{2} = 1 + U^{2} - 2 m / r. \tag {b}
$$
[Hint: Use $\mathbf{G}_{\mathfrak{t}}^{\mathfrak{t}} = -8\pi \rho$ and $\mathbf{G}_{\mathbf{R}}^{\mathbf{t}} = 0$ (Problem 9.20).]
origin.solution:
(b) From $\mathbf{G}_{\hat{\mathbf{t}}}^{\hat{\mathbf{t}}}=-8 \pi \rho$ (see Problem 9.20) we have
$$
8 \pi \rho r^{2}=1-r^{\prime 2} e^{-2 \Lambda}+\dot{r}^{2} e^{-2 \Phi}+2 r \dot{r} e^{-2 \Phi} \dot{\Lambda}+2 r e^{-2 \Lambda}\left(r^{\prime} \Lambda^{\prime}-r^{\prime \prime}\right).
$$
Now use $\mathbf{G}_{\widehat{\mathbf{R}}}^{\widehat{\mathbf{t}}}=0$ to eliminate $\dot{\Lambda}$ , and note that $8 \pi r^{2} \rho r'$ is an exact differential with respect to $\mathbf{R}$
$$
\begin{aligned}
8 \pi \rho r^{2} r^{\prime} &=r^{\prime}-\left(r^{\prime}\right)^{3} e^{-2 \Lambda}+\dot{r}^{2} r^{\prime} e^{-2 \Phi}+2 r \dot{r} e^{-2 \Phi}\left(\dot{r}^{\prime}-\dot{r} \Phi^{\prime}\right) \\
& +2 \mathrm{rr}^{\prime} \mathrm {e}^{-2 \Lambda}\left(\mathrm {r}^{\prime} \Lambda^{\prime}-\mathrm {r}^{\prime \prime}\right) \\
& =\left[r-e^{-2 \Lambda} r\left(r^{\prime}\right)^{2}+r\left(\dot{r}\right)^{2} e^{-2 \Phi}\right]^{\prime}.
\end{aligned}
$$
Integration gives the desired relation
$$
\begin{aligned}
2 m &=r\left(1-e^{-2 \Lambda}\left(r^{\prime}\right)^{2}+\dot{r}^{2} e^{-2 \Phi}\right) \\
& =\mathrm {r}(1-\Gamma^{2}+\mathrm {U}^{2}).
\end{aligned}
$$
